findings:
Today's report (4/27) extends and structurally codifies the prediction surface from the past week, with **eight predictions hitting Relevance 5**: secret-leakage-through-CI-and-agents (4/20) lands the foundational simple-git RCE + Future #2's CISA/NIST sub-catalog framing; GGUF supply chains (4/21) holds with SGLang still unpatched and the CSO Online cross-vendor "copy-paste vulnerability" framing; OAuth-trust + SSO-AI-extension (4/22) is at T-3 days to GA with Okta + Operant + Salt Security's 48.9%-cannot-monitor data — and Future #3 directly extends it to a state-level statutory layer; the Agent Control Plane hyperscaler battleground (4/23) is Big-3-confirmed with Okta + Google Cloud + AWS Bedrock visible the same day; the three-cloud-oligopoly capacity-capture (4/25) is anchored by ~$650B-$700B Mag 7 capex framing + the explicit anchor-deal naming in Future #1; the MCP-protocol attack surface (4/25) sees both threat (SGLang/simple-git/Antigravity/Google IPI 32%) and defense (Okta Agent Gateway/Operant/Google remote-MCP) surge in the same news cycle and is restated by Future #2; the hyperscaler-AI-lab capital-coupling stack-lock-in (4/26) carries forward via Project Glasswing + Mag 7 capex + Hermes-on-Bedrock; and the inference-server primitive-supply-chain LLM-INFRA-CWE class (4/26) is directly restated with sharper specificity by today's Future #2 against a 5-CVE backdrop.

**Five predictions hit Relevance 3-4**: the 5-pole hyperscaler-frontier-lab regime (4/21) at 4 via Project Glasswing + Mag 7 framing; the 27B-Dense + 1M-context standard (4/23) at 4 via Qwen3.6 official page + Apidog roundup but no second-vendor entrant; the 1M-context-default-open-weight era (4/24) at 4 via DeepSeek V4 + Qwen3.6 dual-track; local-first cloud-overflow inversion (4/20) at 3 via DeepSeek V4 + Qwen3.6 + Bonsai cumulative without a new structural step; proprietary vs open-weight split (4/21) at 3 via DeepSeek V4 + Simon Willison + MIT Tech Review carry-forward without a new pricing move; Physical-AI-as-RaaS (4/22) at 3 via Boston Dynamics 30k/year + Tesla Optimus V3; Physical-AI-8h production runs (4/24) at 3 via Boston Dynamics staged plan + Tesla V3 timeline; Physical-AI league tables at IROS / GTC (4/25) at 3 via the same; third-party-vendor-boundary + URL-naming-entropy (4/23) at 3 via the Mythos contractor-URL-guessing saga revival + Altman-vs-Anthropic + CNBC public opinion + Future #3's state-statute extension; consumer-GPU coding agent ≥30% by Q3 2026 (4/26) at 3 via Qwen3.6 + DeepSeek V4 + Bonsai + Foundry Local GA without a Big-3 IDE local-first-toggle commit.

**Three predictions sit at Relevance 1-2**: Headless Everything (4/20) at 1 because Hermes Agent v0.11.0's headline Ink TUI rewrite reinforces the OS-level vector against the headless-metadata thesis; Training-vs-inference SKU split (4/22) at 1 because no MAIA / MTIA dual-track announcements (Cerebras IPO is adjacent, not on-thesis); and Capital-markets-reset for AI-agent-displaced SaaS (4/24) at 2 because today's Mag 7 framing is "AI ROI reckoning" / "rewarding spending on faith → demanding tangible profit" rather than the predicted Workday / Atlassian / Box / Smartsheet 15%+ chained falls — and Motley Fool's "Great Rotation Is Reversing" actually argues the opposite vector.

The dominant structural picture today: today's three predictions (Mag 7 AI-ROI reckoning + AI-Infra CVE class graduating to regulatory primitive + public-opinion-blowback → state statutory identity for autonomous agents) are essentially next-step extensions of last week's predictions. The capital-coupling thesis (4/21, 4/25, 4/26), the inference-server-CVE-class thesis (4/19, 4/21, 4/26), and the OAuth/Agent-Control-Plane/Okta cluster (4/22, 4/23) all snap into the Apr 28-30 window: AI Dev 26 SF opens Apr 28-29 at Pier 48 with 3,000+ devs; Mag 7 prints Apr 29-30; Okta for AI Agents goes GA Apr 30. Three threads predicted across 4/20-4/26 converge on the same 72-hour window.
relation_to_my_preds:
Two of three personal predictions get continued strong corroboration today; the third has no direct hit.

**Malicious local LLMs functioning like malware → strong continuing corroboration.** The SGLang CVE-2026-5760 case remains the canonical example: a model file (GGUF) carries executable code (Jinja2 SSTI in `tokenizer.chat_template`) that fires inside the inference server, and v0.5.10 ships without the recommended `ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment` mitigation — meaning the GGUF-as-malware vector remains live in production and the upstream is not addressing it. CSO Online's "copy-paste vulnerability hits AI inference frameworks at Meta, Nvidia, and Microsoft" generalizes the exact failure mode you predicted across vendors. The zero-trust direction you predicted as the stable response is now visibly converging: Okta for AI Agents GA in 3 days (Agent Discovery + Agent Gateway + credential vaulting + universal logout), Operant AI's Zero-Trust MCP Gateway joining the Okta Integration Network, Google Cloud's remote-MCP-server-support GA + Wiz integration, Salt Security framing "you cannot secure AI without securing the APIs that power it" against 48.9% of orgs cannot monitor AI-agent traffic. Today's Future #3 extends this to the regulatory layer: a US-state Statutory Identity for Autonomous Agents Act mirroring Okta's Agent Discovery + Agent Gateway pattern by Q3 2026 — the "intentionally design system paths AI alone cannot access" architecture moving from product into law. On the harness-engineering / behavior-monitoring side, your "monitor and manage will attract only short-term attention" framing remains consistent with the Pillar Antigravity case (Secure Mode bypass) and the SGLang non-patch — defensive harnesses keep being bypassed at the primitive level.

**Cloud-API-for-advanced + local-LLM-for-routine division → continued direct corroboration.** Today reinforces the cost-pressure half of your prediction without a new pricing move: the **Mag 7 capex framing as "AI ROI reckoning"** — combined 2026 capex from Alphabet $175B-$185B + Amazon ~$200B + Meta $115B-$135B = ~$650B-$700B against Wall Street demanding tangible profit, with goMarkets framing it as "rewarding spending on faith → demanding tangible profit" — is exactly the energy + compute cost-pressure dynamic you forecast as the trigger for SaaS pricing changes. The local-side substitutes continue to deepen: DeepSeek V4-Pro at $1.74 / $3.48 per M tokens with V4-Pro Hugging Face page live and Simon Willison anchoring practitioner read, Qwen3.6-27B running on a single RTX 3090 at 40 tok/s, PrismML Bonsai-8B 1-bit running natively on iPhone 17 Pro Max at 44 tok/s. **Hermes Agent v0.11.0** is the practitioner-facing crystallization of the hybrid pattern your prediction described: pluggable transport architecture under every provider, 5 new inference paths, native AWS Bedrock + GPT-5.5 via Codex OAuth (cloud routes for advanced tasks) running alongside local-runtime self-host options (routine tasks) — the division-of-labor harness, not a binary choice. Today's Mag 7 print window (Apr 29-30) is the next observation point for whether the SaaS-pricing-structure shift you anticipated will appear in capex commentary.

**RL/LLM-based forecasting performance for real-world phenomena → no direct hit today.** Today's news has no items on RL-for-forecasting research, finance/weather/policy LLM forecasters, or methodology benchmarks for LLM-driven prediction systems. The closest adjacent — Simon Willison's Apr 27 "Vibe coding SwiftUI apps" piece + Apr 24 DeepSeek V4 review — are practitioner-quality reads, not forecasting-methodology pieces. AI Dev 26 SF Apr 28-29 (Andrew Ng-hosted at Pier 48) lists "AI Governance" and "Coding-with-AI" tracks but no forecasting/evals track from the public agenda. Stanford's **Causal Science Conference: Frontiers in AI Evaluation (Apr 29, 12-6 PM PT, Simonyi Conference Center)** is the closest near-term event that could surface signals on the RL-forecasting axis, but no preview content today. Carry-only.
